source_level1f3_analysis.protocol_amendment: Held-out evaluation narrowed to 128 primary / 64 stress / 64 easy trials after observing that the official IBM BCF implementation incurred high end-to-end cost under repeated independently materialized contextual-subset benchmark calls. This amendment preserves the primary cell at full requested size while bounding overnight runtime.

iteration_cap_selection.selection_objective: max exact_recovery, then min iterations, then min factorization time

selected_bcf_config.source_note: Official F=3, B=4, D=512 hyperparameter table at codebook size 10. Reused as the nearest official single-product prior for M=5 and M=10.

provenance_audit.bug_summary: A Level 1F.3 execution bug allowed the selected BCF iteration cap to drop when the chosen configuration was reconstructed from the uncapped candidate list. Intermediate debugging runs therefore fell back to the task-derived cap instead of the frozen cap=16.
pre_fix_artifacts: transient partial held-out trial files created during debugging
provenance_audit.status_rationale: The final committed Level 1F.3 artifacts were regenerated with the frozen cap=16 and without changing BCF configuration, selector logic, or held-out seed ranges after the bug was fixed. However, held-out outcomes were observed during debugging before the final rerun, so the substrate comparison remains provisional for publication purposes.
provenance_audit.publication_requirement: Fresh clean-seed confirmation is required before publication-level substrate claims.

timing_audit.measurement_scope: heldout trials plus timing summary artifact
timing_audit.repeats_note: Level 1F.3 artifacts did not serialize timing repeats; Level 1F.4 therefore reports repeats as unavailable in-artifact.

memory_audit.caveat: Peak CUDA memory reflects allocator-visible runtime behavior in the audited harness and should not be treated as a pure codebook-state comparison.

config_sweep_summary.selection_note: Selected among the tested upstream-justified configurations on the frozen development split.
Selected among the tested upstream-justified configurations on the frozen development split.
Selected among the tested upstream-justified configurations on the frozen development split.

claim_a_context_portability.wording: Semantic context beat size-matched random candidate selection on truth inclusion and exact recovery for MAP and official IBM BCF in the tested single-product cells.

claim_b_factorizer_internal_semantic_ben.wording: A substrate-internal semantic benefit beyond truth retention was not consistently demonstrated across MAP and BCF.

claim_c_map_stronger_than_bcf.wording: MAP was stronger than the audited official IBM BCF implementation in the tested operating envelope.

claim_d_external_context_transfer.wording: The external context-selection mechanism transferred across two representation/factorizer families.

allowed_claims: Semantic context beats random candidate selection.
The external context-selection mechanism transferred from MAP to official IBM BCF.
MAP was stronger than official IBM BCF in the tested single-product operating envelope.
forbidden_claims: Verifier, fallback policy, or open-world lifecycle already transferred to BCF.
MAP is generally superior to all block-code factorizers.
